betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Vacuum Chamber: Nucleosynthesis Chamber Controller§f

§aThe Nucleosynthesis Chamber is the heaviest QMD multiblock — it triggers nuclear fusion and exotic synthesis.§f
The §bNucleosynthesis Chamber Controller§f (`qmd:nucleosynthesis_chamber_controller`) is the brain of a §bNucleosynthesis Chamber§f multiblock, built on a §bVacuum Chamber§f hull. It is the §bbiggest and most powerful§f QMD reaction chamber — used to trigger §bmuon-catalyzed fusion§f, §bisotope synthesis§f, and §bexotic particle production§f.

§6Controller Crafting Recipe (QMDCraftingRecipeHandler.java:220)§f:
Per `addShapedOreRecipe(QMDBlocks.nucleosynthesisChamberController, ...)`:
- Output: §bNucleosynthesis Chamber Controller§f
- Pattern (3×3): §bP-E-P / B-F-B / P-E-P§f
  - §bP§f = §bElite Plate§f
  - §bE§f = §bSuper Alloy Ingot§f
  - §bB§f = §bElite Processor§f
  - §bF§f = §bVacuum Chamber Casing§f

§6Hull Structure (VacuumChamberLogic.java:35)§f:
The controller sits on top of a §bVacuum Chamber§f hull:
- §bInterior§f: 5×5×5 to 7×7×7 (exterior 7×7×7 to 9×9×9)
- §bHull§f: §bVacuum Chamber Casing§f and §bVacuum Chamber Glass§f
- §bInternal Coils§f: §bVacuum Chamber Coils§f (BSCCO + Osmiridium) — these are §bessential§f for confining plasma
- §bBeam Ports§f: §bVacuum Chamber Beam Ports§f for particle in/out
- §bEnergy Ports§f: §bVacuum Chamber Energy Ports§f
- §bCoolant Vents§f: §bVacuum Chamber Vents§f
- §bHeaters§f: §bVacuum Chamber Heaters§f (various types — see the §bVacuum Chamber Heaters & Mercury Loops§f quest) — used to preheat the chamber for plasma ignition

§6Example Recipe — Muon-Catalyzed Fusion (NucleosynthesisChamberRecipes.java:37)§f:
Per `addRecipe(fluidStack("hydrogen", 500), fluidStack("boron_11", 144), new ParticleStack(Particles.muon, ...), ...)`:
- Fluid Input 1: §bHydrogen§f (500 mB)
- Fluid Input 2: §bBoron-11 fluid§f (144 mB)
- Particle Input: §bmuon particle§f
- Output 1: §bmuon particle§f (catalyst recovered)
- Output 2: §bHelium-4 fluid§f (3000 mB)
- Output 3: empty
- §bRF cost§f: 1,000,000,000 RF (1 Giga-RF)
- §bEnergy released§f: 8,680,000 (H)

This is the §bcanonical§f muon-catalyzed fusion reaction: deuterium + deuterium → helium-3 + neutron, but here realized as hydrogen + boron-11 + muon catalyst → helium.

§6Other Recipe Categories§f:
- §bIsotope synthesis§f: neutron + target isotope → heavier isotope
- §bExotic particle production§f: high-energy photon + target → tau leptons, glueballs, etc.
- §bAntimatter synthesis§f: antiproton + positron → anti-hydrogen (per §bExotic Containment Chamber§f recipes)

§6Component Supply Chain§f:
- §bElite Processor§f → requires §bAdvanced Processor§f + BSCCO + Hafnium + Platinum (QMDRecipes.java:505)
- §bSuper Alloy Ingot§f → end-game alloy from §bNC Alloy Furnace§f
- §bVacuum Chamber Casing§f → from §bNC Manufactory§f (Osmiridium + Tough + Steel + Frame)

§cWarning§f: The §b1 Giga-RF cost§f of muon-catalyzed fusion is enormous — the §bNucleosynthesis Chamber§f must be fed by §bmassive power storage§f (e.g. multiple §bRfToolspower Powercells§f). The chamber also requires §bplasma-grade heat management§f — use a §bMercury Loop§f connected to the chamber's fluid ports.

§eTip§f: A §b7×7×7§f hull (interior 5×5×5) is the smallest legal size and ideal for first synthesis runs. Build a §b§eMercury Loop§f §f first using a §bVacuum Chamber Heater (Mercury)§f and §bVacuum Chamber Heater (Diamond)§f to preheat the chamber.
